mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with accuracy exceeding 55% in a 10-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any rolling 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the historical correlation between rapid rate hikes and equity drawdowns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising personal savings and financial asset balances indicate increased household liquidity that flows into equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high and low RSAFS growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives consumer demand and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when wage growth data is introduced.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with rising consumer credit balances signals demand-pull inflation that erodes real corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite simultaneous GDP and credit acceleration, or the negative predictive relationship reverses to positive during periods of high inflation.
